document.title: UNI EN ISO 7010: Graphical symbols - Safety colours and safety signs - Registered safety signs
authorityOrJurisdiction: UNI - Ente Nazionale Italiano di Unificazione
document.revisionOrVersion: UNI EN ISO 7010:2012; EN ISO 7010:2012; ISO 7010:2011 Second edition

sourceSummary.oneLine: UNI EN ISO 7010:2012 is the Italian national adoption, in English, of EN ISO 7010:2012 and ISO 7010:2011 for registered safety signs.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Use as a standard source for prescribed registered safety signs for accident prevention, fire protection, health hazard information and emergency evacuation.
keyPoints: The standard prescribes safety signs for accident prevention, fire protection, health hazard information and emergency evacuation.
Safety sign shape and colour are according to ISO 3864-1, and graphical symbol design is according to ISO 3864-3.
It applies to locations where safety issues related to people need to be addressed, excluding rail, road, river, maritime and air traffic signalling and other regulated sectors that may differ.
The safety sign categories are E safe condition signs, F fire equipment signs, M mandatory action signs, P prohibition signs and W warning signs.

Where a registered safety sign is unavailable, the meaning is obtained by developing a new sign using ISO 3864-1 and ISO 3864-3 or by using an appropriate general sign with supplementary safety information.

entities.name: UNI
entities.evidence: UNI Ente Nazionale Italiano di Unificazione

entities.name: CEN
entities.evidence: This European Standard was approved by CEN on 23 June 2012.

entities.name: ISO
entities.evidence: ISO (the International Organization for Standardization) is a worldwide federation of national standards bodies

entities.name: ISO/TC 145
entities.evidence: ISO 7010 was prepared by Technical Committee ISO/TC 145, Graphical symbols, Subcommittee SC 2, Safety identification, signs, shapes, symbols and colours.

entities.name: CEN-CENELEC Management Centre
entities.evidence: Up-to-date lists and bibliographical references concerning such national standards may be obtained on application to the CEN-CENELEC Management Centre

concepts.name: Registered safety signs
concepts.evidence: This International Standard prescribes safety signs for the purposes of accident prevention, fire protection, health hazard information and emergency evacuation.

concepts.name: Safety sign
concepts.evidence: sign giving a general safety message, obtained by a combination of a colour and geometric shape and which, by the addition of a graphical symbol, gives a particular safety message

concepts.name: Safety sign original
concepts.evidence: safety sign with which a referent, a graphical representation and a description of the application are associated

concepts.name: Referent
concepts.evidence: idea or object that a graphical symbol is intended to represent

concepts.name: Supplementary sign
concepts.evidence: sign that is supportive of another sign and the main purpose of which is to provide additional clarification

concepts.name: Safety sign categories
concepts.evidence: Safety signs are categorized according to their function as follows: E is the category for signs indicating an evacuation route... F is the category for fire equipment signs; M is the category for mandatory action signs; P is the category for prohibition signs; W is the category for warning signs.

evidence.claim: UNI EN ISO 7010:2012 is the official English-language Italian version of EN ISO 7010, July 2012 edition.
evidence.evidenceText: La presente norma è la versione ufficiale in lingua inglese della norma europea EN ISO 7010 (edizione luglio 2012).

evidence.claim: The Italian national standard entered the UNI national standards body on 18 October 2012.
evidence.evidenceText: La presente norma è stata ratificata dal Presidente dell’UNI ed è entrata a far parte del corpo normativo nazionale il 18 ottobre 2012.

evidence.claim: EN ISO 7010:2012 was approved by CEN on 23 June 2012.
evidence.evidenceText: This European Standard was approved by CEN on 23 June 2012.

evidence.claim: The text of ISO 7010:2011 was taken over as EN ISO 7010:2012.
evidence.evidenceText: The text of ISO 7010:2011 has been prepared by Technical Committee ISO/TC 145 “Graphical symbols” of the International Organization for Standardization (ISO) and has been taken over as EN ISO 7010:2012.

evidence.claim: The European endorsement approved ISO 7010:2011 as EN ISO 7010:2012 without modification.
evidence.evidenceText: The text of ISO 7010:2011 has been approved by CEN as a EN ISO 7010:2012 without any modification.

evidence.claim: ISO 7010:2011 is the second edition of the international standard.
evidence.evidenceText: ISO 7010 Second edition 2011-06-ij Graphical symbols — Safety colours and safety signs — Registered safety signs

evidence.claim: ISO 7010:2011 cancels and replaces ISO 7010:2003 and ISO 6309:1987 and incorporates amendments 1 through 8.
evidence.evidenceText: This second edition cancels and replaces the first edition (ISO 7010:2003), as well as ISO 6309:1987. It also incorporates the Amendments ISO 7010:2003/Amd. 1:2006... ISO 7010:2003/Amd. 8:2011.

evidence.claim: ISO 7010 prescribes safety signs for accident prevention, fire protection, health hazard information and emergency evacuation.
evidence.evidenceText: This International Standard prescribes safety signs for the purposes of accident prevention, fire protection, health hazard information and emergency evacuation.

evidence.claim: The shape and colour of each ISO 7010 safety sign are according to ISO 3864-1.
evidence.evidenceText: The shape and colour of each safety sign are according to ISO 3864-1

evidence.claim: The design of ISO 7010 graphical symbols is according to ISO 3864-3.
evidence.evidenceText: the design of the graphical symbols is according to ISO 3864-3.

evidence.claim: ISO 7010 applies to locations where safety issues related to people need to be addressed.
evidence.evidenceText: This International Standard is applicable to all locations where safety issues related to people need to be addressed.

evidence.claim: ISO 7010 does not apply to signalling for rail, road, river, maritime and air traffic or sectors subject to differing regulation.
evidence.evidenceText: it is not applicable to the signalling used for guiding rail, road, river, maritime and air traffic and, in general, to those sectors subject to a regulation which may differ

evidence.claim: ISO 7010 specifies safety sign originals that may be scaled for reproduction and application purposes.
evidence.evidenceText: This International Standard specifies the safety sign originals that may be scaled for reproduction and application purposes.

evidence.claim: Registered ISO 7010 safety signs have unique reference numbers and are maintained electronically through a database.
evidence.evidenceText: This International Standard will be maintained electronically through the use of a database... with each registered safety sign having a unique reference number

evidence.claim: ISO 7010 safety sign categories are E, F, M, P and W.
evidence.evidenceText: E is the category for signs indicating an evacuation route... F is the category for fire equipment signs; M is the category for mandatory action signs; P is the category for prohibition signs; W is the category for warning signs.

evidence.claim: Registered safety signs should be reproduced exactly as shown, with limited graphical modification allowed when meaning and comprehension are retained.
evidence.evidenceText: The safety signs should be reproduced exactly as shown in Tables 3 to 7. However some degree of graphical modification is permitted when cultural differences or special application formats need to be considered, provided the original meaning is retained and comprehension of the safety sign is maintained.

evidence.claim: If no registered safety sign exists for a desired meaning, a new sign should follow ISO 3864-1 and ISO 3864-3 or an appropriate general sign with supplementary safety information should be used.
evidence.evidenceText: Where a registered safety sign is not available... the meaning shall be obtained either by developing a new safety sign following the design principles of ISO 3864-1 and ISO 3864-3 or by using the appropriate general sign... together with supplementary safety information.

reviewFlags.reason: No product models are present; source metadata productSignal is Unclassified.
The ISO publication date line appears OCR-corrupted as '2011-06-ij', so only the year/month and second edition wording are extracted with medium confidence.
The source combines UNI national adoption text, CEN European foreword and ISO international standard text; authority is therefore multi-level rather than a single regulator.
